content.claim: Current enrollment in 6- and 8-year gimnázium tracks is 9–10% of all Hungarian students, whereas 85% of 15-year-old Hungarian students attend schools that select on performance according to OECD 2019 data; these two figures measure different phenomena (track-specific enrollment vs. systemic selectivity) and cannot be directly compared as measures of the same problem.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: The two metrics describe different levels of analysis and are presented as conflicting scales in political debate rather than reconciled in a single empirical study
The 85% figure appears in an opinion piece and requires verification against original OECD sources
No source clarifies what fraction of the 85% selectivity is attributable to the 6/8-year tracks vs. other tracking mechanisms
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
